title: Epigram 2. - Kissing Helena.
content:
FROM THE GREEK OF PLATO.

    Kissing Helena, together
    With my kiss, my soul beside it
    Came to my lips, and there I kept it, -
    For the poor thing had wandered thither,
    To follow where the kiss should guide it,
    Oh, cruel I, to intercept it!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Percy Bysshe Shelley delivers a powerful performance in "Epigram 2. - Kissing Helena."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
